description: The Guadalajara Cartel was the first exclusively drug-trafficking criminal organization in Mexico, formed around 1978 by Miguel Ángel Félix Gallardo, Rafael Caro Quintero, and Ernesto Fonseca Carrillo. It controlled nearly all drug trafficking operations in Mexico during the late 1970s and 1980s, trafficking opium, marijuana, and cocaine to the United States. The cartel was dismantled in 1989 following the arrest of its leaders.

country_links.evidence_quote: Félix Gallardo comenzó a trabajar con los cárteles colombianos , empezando con el cartel de Cali y el cartel de Medellín
country_links.context: The cartel partnered with Colombian cartels to receive and transport cocaine from Colombia.

country_links.evidence_quote: habría permitido a Caro Quintero huir del aeropuerto de Guadalajara, en un jet privado, para buscar refugio en Costa Rica
country_links.context: A cartel leader fled to Costa Rica after being allowed to escape.

country_links.evidence_quote: con el objetivo de transportar opio , marihuana , y posteriormente, cocaína a los Estados Unidos
country_links.context: The cartel transported drugs to the United States and used the Mexico-U.S. border as a trafficking route.
